name: HealthKit
promptInjection:
## HealthKit — authorization and read flow

**Availability:** Check HKHealthStore.isHealthDataAvailable() before using HealthKit.

**Request authorization:** Call healthStore.requestAuthorization(toShare:read:completion:) with the exact types you need (e.g. for steps: read set containing HKQuantityType(.stepCount)). Use a single HKHealthStore instance (e.g. @StateObject or shared); do not create a new HKHealthStore on every view update.

**Critical — check status and run query in the completion handler:** Do NOT check authorization status or decide "denied" before the completion handler runs. The completion handler is called after the user dismisses the Health permission sheet. Inside the completion handler: (1) Check authorization only there — for read-only data like steps, you cannot know if the user granted read (HealthKit does not expose read grant/deny). So immediately run your read query (e.g. HKSampleQuery or HKStatisticsQuery for step count) in the completion handler; do not require an app restart. (2) For write/sharing, use authorizationStatus(for: type) and only treat .sharingAuthorized as authorized. (3) Show "Health Access Denied" only when the user has explicitly denied — i.e. when authorizationStatus(for: type) is .sharingDenied for a type you write to, or when you have a clear denied outcome. Do NOT show "Health Access Denied" when status is .notDetermined (user has not decided yet or sheet just dismissed). For read-only apps: after the sheet dismisses, run the query; if the query returns no data, show a neutral message like "No step data" or "Enable Steps in Health" rather than "Health Access Denied" unless you are certain the user denied.

**Flow:** On view appear, request authorization; in the completion handler (on main queue), run the step count (or other) query and update UI with the result. No app restart required.

**Plist:** // REQUIRES PLIST: NSHealthShareUsageDescription (for reading), NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription (for writing). In summary, warn: "Enable HealthKit in your App ID in the Apple Developer portal."

**Common types:** Steps: HKQuantityType(.stepCount); heart rate: HKQuantityType(.heartRate); calories: HKQuantityType(.activeEnergyBurned); sleep: HKCategoryType(.sleepAnalysis); distance: HKQuantityType(.distanceWalkingRunning).
promptBlock: HealthKit: isHealthDataAvailable() first; single HKHealthStore. Request auth with requestAuthorization(toShare:read:completion:). In the completion handler (not before): run the read query immediately so data appears without app restart. Show "Health Access Denied" only when user explicitly denied (.sharingDenied), never for .notDetermined. For read-only steps: run step query in completion handler; if no data, show "No step data" not "Denied". REQUIRES PLIST: NSHealthShareUsageDescription, NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription.
antiPatterns: Checking authorization status before the requestAuthorization completion handler runs
Showing "Health Access Denied" when HKAuthorizationStatus is .notDetermined
Requiring app restart after the user grants permission — run the read query inside the completion handler
Creating a new HKHealthStore() on every view update or request

commonErrors: 'nil' cannot be used in context expecting type 'Int'
